metadata.guest_bio: Soraya Azahari is a Senior Engineer specializing in water engineering with extensive experience in environmental and wastewater consulting. She is passionate about mentorship and career development in civil engineering.
metadata.definition_answer: Mentorship in engineering involves experienced professionals guiding others to develop technical skills, confidence, and career advancement. It encompasses peer support, technical coaching, and career development to foster growth and mastery in the engineering field.
metadata.episode_intro: Mentorship engineering growth is essential for building confidence, technical expertise, and leadership skills in civil engineering. This episode explores practical mentorship strategies that elevate both individual careers and organizational success. Listeners will gain insights on combining technical and career mentorship to achieve professional growth.
metadata.episode_summary: This episode discusses the critical role of mentorship in engineering growth, highlighting three types of mentorship: peer, technical, and career. It covers how mentorship builds essential skills, confidence, and leadership abilities while improving design constructability through collaboration with construction professionals. The conversation also addresses transitioning from mentee to mentor and fostering a mentorship culture within organizations.
metadata.qa_items_0_question: What is mentorship engineering growth and why is it important?
metadata.qa_items_0_answer: Mentorship engineering growth involves guiding engineers through technical and career development to build their confidence, skills, and leadership potential. It is important because it helps professionals navigate complex technical challenges and career transitions, leading to better performance and long-term success.
metadata.qa_items_1_question: How do technical and career mentors complement each other?
metadata.qa_items_1_answer: Technical mentors focus on developing daily work skills, design expectations, and specific technical knowledge, while career mentors provide guidance on long-term career paths, leadership skills, and professional network building. Both types of mentorship together offer a well-rounded support system for engineers.
metadata.qa_items_2_question: How can engineers improve constructability through mentorship?
metadata.qa_items_2_answer: Mentorship from construction professionals helps engineers understand real-world constraints and best practices in project execution. This collaboration informs design decisions, making them more practical and constructible, ultimately enhancing project outcomes and client satisfaction.
metadata.qa_items_3_question: What should early career engineers focus on beyond technical skills?
metadata.qa_items_3_answer: Early career engineers should develop soft skills like communication, confidence, and work style awareness. Learning to manage expectations, engage in open dialogue, and advocate for themselves are critical to successful career advancement.
metadata.qa_items_4_question: What are practical steps to find mentorship if a formal program is unavailable?
metadata.qa_items_4_answer: Engineers should be open about their needs, seek mentors both inside and outside their firms, engage in professional organizations, attend conferences, and leverage networking platforms like LinkedIn to connect with potential mentors across different disciplines and industries.
metadata.qa_items_5_question: How can firms foster a culture of organic mentorship?
metadata.qa_items_5_answer: Firms can create spaces and opportunities for employees to connect across disciplines and levels, promote a collaborative culture, and provide platforms for knowledge sharing. Encouraging natural relationships and prioritizing meaningful connections helps mentorship thrive organically.
metadata.qa_items_6_question: When do engineers transition from mentee to mentor?
metadata.qa_items_6_answer: The transition often occurs when engineers take on supervisory roles or begin delegating tasks, signaling a shift to providing guidance and support to others. It can also be a gradual shift marked by sharing experience and advice to help peers and junior colleagues grow.
metadata.qa_items_7_question: How does mentorship help overcome imposter syndrome in early careers?
metadata.qa_items_7_answer: Mentorship provides support, reassurance, and feedback that help build confidence. Having mentors to walk alongside, provide encouragement, and share knowledge reduces anxiety and feelings of inadequacy common among early career engineers.
metadata.mid_cta_body: Explore EMI training to develop mentorship abilities that boost technical competence, leadership, and career progression in engineering. Empower yourself and your team with proven techniques.
metadata.footer_cta_body: Join EMI programs to advance your mentorship and coaching skills. Build stronger, more confident engineering teams that deliver exceptional results.

metadata.aeo_intro_html: Episode TCEP 310: Mentorship engineering growth is essential for building confidence, technical expertise, and leadership skills in civil engineering. This episode explores practical mentorship strategies that elevate both individual careers and organizational success. Listeners will gain insights on combining technical and career mentorship to achieve professional growth.
metadata.aeo_definition_html:
What is Mentorship in Engineering?

Mentorship in engineering involves experienced professionals guiding others to develop technical skills, confidence, and career advancement. It encompasses peer support, technical coaching, and career development to foster growth and mastery in the engineering field.
metadata.aeo_qa_html:
What is mentorship engineering growth and why is it important?

Mentorship engineering growth involves guiding engineers through technical and career development to build their confidence, skills, and leadership potential. It is important because it helps professionals navigate complex technical challenges and career transitions, leading to better performance and long-term success.
  • Builds technical and leadership skills
  • Enhances confidence and critical thinking
  • Supports career progression and role transitions

How do technical and career mentors complement each other?

Technical mentors focus on developing daily work skills, design expectations, and specific technical knowledge, while career mentors provide guidance on long-term career paths, leadership skills, and professional network building. Both types of mentorship together offer a well-rounded support system for engineers.
  • Technical mentors guide project-based skills
  • Career mentors focus on growth trajectory
  • Combined mentorship provides holistic support

How can engineers improve constructability through mentorship?

Mentorship from construction professionals helps engineers understand real-world constraints and best practices in project execution. This collaboration informs design decisions, making them more practical and constructible, ultimately enhancing project outcomes and client satisfaction.
  • Gain insights on real-world construction challenges
  • Apply lessons learned to the design process
  • Enhance communication between design and construction teams

What should early-career engineers focus on beyond technical skills?

Early-career engineers should develop soft skills like communication, confidence, and work style awareness. Learning to manage expectations, engage in open dialogue, and advocate for themselves are critical to successful career advancement.
  • Develop effective communication and engagement
  • Build confidence through mentorship and experience
  • Understand different work styles and collaboration

What are practical steps to find mentorship if a formal program is unavailable?

Engineers should be open about their needs, seek mentors both inside and outside their firms, engage in professional organizations, attend conferences, and leverage networking platforms like LinkedIn to connect with potential mentors across different disciplines and industries.
  • Communicate mentorship needs clearly
  • Participate in industry events and committees
  • Use online networks to expand connections

How can firms foster a culture of organic mentorship?

Firms can create spaces and opportunities for employees to connect across disciplines and levels, promote a collaborative culture, and provide platforms for knowledge sharing. Encouraging natural relationships and prioritizing meaningful connections helps mentorship thrive organically.
  • Facilitate cross-team and cross-level interactions
  • Encourage collaboration and communication
  • Support career and technical growth initiatives

When do engineers transition from mentee to mentor?

The transition often occurs when engineers take on supervisory roles or begin delegating tasks, signaling a shift to providing guidance and support to others. It can also be a gradual shift marked by sharing experience and advice to help peers and junior colleagues grow.
  • Typically starts with supervisory responsibilities
  • Involves delegating and guiding work
  • Growing focus on supporting others' professional growth

How does mentorship help overcome imposter syndrome in early careers?

Mentorship provides support, reassurance, and feedback that help build confidence. Having mentors to walk alongside, provide encouragement, and share knowledge reduces anxiety and feelings of inadequacy common among early-career engineers.
  • Creates a supportive learning environment
  • Encourages critical thinking and taking initiative
  • Builds confidence through shared experiences and feedback

metadata.aeo_speakers_html:
Meet the Speakers

Your Host

K. James Taylor, P.E.

K. James Taylor, Jr., P.E., is a licensed professional engineer and an associate vice president at Verdantas, an emerging environmental, engineering, and technical consulting company with a green, sustainable, and people-first approach in the foreground. James has over 19 years of experience in civil engineering in the land development and municipal fields. James has served as a project manager since 2018. In 2021, James was recognized with the Outstanding Project Manager Award at Duffield Associates (now Verdantas) for outstanding performance as a project manager and his consistent display of leadership traits valued by the company, including scheduling, management, proactive communication, collaboration, responsiveness, and client-focused service. James was selected as the 2021 Young Engineer of the Year by the American Society of Civil Engineers (ASCE) Delaware Section and is a Delaware Business Times 40 Under 40 2024 honoree. He also serves as the president of the Delaware Engineering Society and on the National Society of Professional Engineers Board of Directors as the New Professionals Director from 2021 to 2023 and currently as the Northeast Region Director. James recently joined the Board of Directors for ACE Mentor Delaware, a no-cost after-school program designed to connect high school students with professionals in the Architecture, Construction, and Engineering (ACE) industry.
Guest Expert

Soraya Azahari, PE

Senior Engineer at Parametrix
Soraya Azahari, P.E. is a Senior Engineer specializing in water and wastewater infrastructure and process mechanical design. Based in Portland, Oregon, she currently leads multidisciplinary projects at Parametrix, where she’s been instrumental in mentoring early-career professionals and advancing complex engineering solutions since 2022. In recognition of her leadership, collaboration, and impact, Soraya was selected as the firm’s 2025 Employee-Owner of the Year by her peers and leadership team. She holds a B.S. in Environmental Engineering from the University of Delaware and a master’s in Chemical & Environmental Engineering from Universiti Putra Malaysia, and brings global field experience and a passion for resilient infrastructure to her work.

metadata.videotitle: The Secret to Engineering Success It's Mentorship!
metadata.videodesc: In this video, Soraya Azahari, PE, shares how mentorship plays a critical role in shaping successful engineers. She explains the difference between career mentors and technical mentors, and why learning from experienced professionals—especially those in construction—can help engineers design better, more practical solutions.
metadata._yoast_wpseo_opengraph-description: Learn practical strategies for mentorship engineering growth and how mentors help engineers build confidence, skills, and stronger careers.
